title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 03, 2026: US Markets Closed for Holiday, Dow Hits Record, Tech Sags

items.headline: US Markets Closed for Independence Day Holiday; Dow Reaches New High, Nasdaq Falls Amid Chip Sell-off on Thursday
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are closed today, July 3, 2026, in observance of the Independence Day holiday. On Thursday, July 2, the Dow Jones Industrial Average surged to a new record high, gaining 1.1%, while the S&P 500 closed virtually flat. The Nasdaq Composite, however, experienced a notable decline of 0.8%, primarily driven by a continued sell-off in chipmakers and growing concerns over AI valuations. Market sentiment was influenced by a softer-than-expected US jobs report for June, which saw only 57,000 jobs added, tempering expectations for near-term Federal Reserve rate hikes. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Up 594.83 points (+1.1%) to a record high.

items.headline: ClearOne Surges on Trading Day
items.summary: ClearOne led gainers on Thursday's trading session.

items.summary: Surgepays was among the top performers.

items.summary: Elicio Therapeutics faced a sharp decline.

items.summary: Linkhome saw a considerable fall in its stock price.

items.headline: Nocera Falls Amid Selling Pressure
items.summary: Nocera was one of the biggest losers of the day.

items.headline: Tesla Shares Decline Despite Delivery Beat
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) shares fell 7.5% on Thursday, despite the company reporting stronger-than-expected delivery estimates for the second quarter.

items.headline: Nvidia Among Chipmakers Hit by Sell-off
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) fell 1.4% as chipmakers extended their decline amid concerns over AI valuations and a broader tech sell-off.

items.headline: Soft June Jobs Report Tempers Fed Rate Hike Expectations
items.summary: The U.S. economy added a weaker-than-expected 57,000 jobs in June, significantly below the 100,000 anticipated by economists. Wage growth remained slow at 3.5%, trailing the 4.2% inflation rate, while the unemployment rate marginally decreased to 4.2%. This data has led traders to price in an 82% chance that the Federal Reserve, under Chairman Kevin Warsh, will not raise interest rates at its upcoming meeting.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Underway: Healthcare, Financials Lead; Tech Falters
items.summary: A significant shift in market leadership is evident, with defensive sectors gaining traction. Healthcare has moved into the leading quadrant, joining Utilities, Industrials, and Financials, as investors favor earnings stability. Conversely, the Technology sector has rotated into the lagging quadrant, losing relative strength and momentum, largely due to the ongoing chipmaker sell-off and AI valuation concerns. Energy remains the weakest sector.

items.headline: AI Bubble Fears Mount as Valuations Questioned
items.summary: Concerns about an 'AI bubble' are growing, with some investors drawing parallels to past market frenzies. The cyclically adjusted price-to-earnings (CAPE) ratio for the S&P 500 is currently elevated at 41.4, a level only seen during the dot-com boom of 1999-2000, suggesting a frothy market environment.

items.headline: Upcoming Earnings Reports on the Horizon
items.summary: While Waldencast (WALD) reported Q1 2026 earnings before market open today, the focus will shift to major companies next week. Key earnings reports expected around July 9-14 include PepsiCo, Delta Air Lines, and several large banks such as Bank of America, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase & Co., Wells Fargo & Company, and Citigroup.
